title: fix: tests webserver syntaxwarning
body:
> Changed in Python version 3.12: Unrecognized escape sequences produce a SyntaxWarning. In a future Python version they will be eventually a SyntaxError.
[2.4.1.1. Escape sequences](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#index-23)

Change to raw string literal to fix:
```
/webserver.py:17: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  elif re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path):
/webserver.py:18: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  result = re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path)
```

step_plan.summary: Fixes Python 3.12+ SyntaxWarning in the test helper web server by switching the /status/<code> regex patterns to raw string literals (preserving existing routing behavior).

steps.operation: Convert the /status/<code> regex strings to raw string literals (and reuse the match object) to avoid Python 3.12+ invalid escape sequence warnings
steps.reason: In Python 3.12+, non-raw string literals containing regex escapes like "\d" emit a SyntaxWarning (and may become an error in the future). Using a raw string (e.g., r"/status/(\d+)") preserves the regex semantics while eliminating the warning. Reusing the match object avoids running the same regex twice.
steps.side_effects: No functional behavior change expected for the /status/<code> route; only affects this test helper server. If any tests implicitly relied on the double match call (unlikely), behavior would still be equivalent. Run the test suite under Python 3.12+ to confirm the warning is gone.
